arabic_text:
قبر
جسم
نفس
صورت
سخت ہڈیاں
نرم حصے
لاہوت
ناسوت
قلب = نفس
english_text:
Grave
Body
Soul
Form
Hard bones
Soft parts
Lahut
Nasut
Heart = Soul
60 Years × 12 Burooj = 720 Years
720 + 280 = 1000 Years
alt_text: A multi-element resurrection diagram. At the top is 'قبر' (Grave) splitting into 'جسم' (Body) and 'نفس' (Soul). The Body branches to 'سخت ہڈیاں' (Hard bones, leading to a treasure chest of jewels) and 'نرم حصے' (Soft parts). The Soul branches via 'صورت' (Form) to two upper figures labeled 'لاہوت' (Divine realm) and 'ناسوت' (Physical realm). A zodiac wheel with 12 numbered constellations (Aries through Pisces) occupies the center, with arrows showing 140 years above and 140 years below. The math '60 Years × 12 Burooj = 720 Years' and '720 + 280 = 1000 Years' is shown on the right, alongside the equation 'قلب = نفس' (Heart = Soul). A human silhouette emerges at the bottom of the diagram.
notes: Major teaching diagram of the 1000-year resurrection cycle: the body decomposes (hard bones to jewels, soft parts dissolve), while the soul ascends through the 12 zodiacal mansions (Burooj) at 60 years each plus 140-year transitions, yielding a total of 1000 years. The dual ascent into Lahut (divine) and Nasut (physical) is the core teaching. The equation 'Heart = Soul' identifies the locus of the soul in the qalb.
